records.ety: [OE. peinten, fr. F. peint, p. p. of peindre to paint, fr. L. pingere, pictum; cf. Gr. poiki`los many-colored, Skr. piç to adorn. Cf. Depict, Picture, Pigment, Pint.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Painted; p. pr. & vb. n. Painting.]

defs: To cover with coloring matter; to apply paint to; as, to paint a house, a signboard, etc.
quotes: Jezebel painted her face and tired her head.

defs: To color, stain, or tinge; to adorn or beautify with colors; to diversify with colors.
quotes: Not painted with the crimson spots of blood.
Cuckoo buds of yellow hue Do paint the meadows with delight.

defs: To form in colors a figure or likeness of on a flat surface, as upon canvas; to represent by means of colors or hues; to exhibit in a tinted image; to portray with paints; as, to paint a portrait or a landscape.
To represent or exhibit to the mind; to describe vividly; to delineate; to image; to depict; as, to paint a political opponent as a traitor.
quotes: Disloyal? The word is too good to paint out her wickedness.
If folly grow romantic, I must paint it.

defs: To practice the art of painting; as, the artist paints well.
To color one's face by way of beautifying it.
quotes: Let her paint an inch thick.

defs: The same prepared with a vehicle, as oil, water with gum, or the like, for application to a surface.
